description: AI-assisted GitHub PR review tool that fetches PR changes, summarizes impact, detects risky code, and generates actionable review suggestions.

userStories.title: Create app foundation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a maintainable FastAPI and frontend project foundation so that the PR review assistant can be built and demonstrated reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a backend service using FastAPI with health check and version endpoints.
Create a frontend app shell with a single PR review workspace page.
Define environment configuration for GitHub token, model provider, model name, and request timeout.
Add typed request and response schemas for review tasks using Pydantic.
Add README setup instructions for local development.
Backend tests pass.
Frontend typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Parse GitHub PR input
userStories.description: As a user, I want to paste a GitHub PR URL so that the system can identify the repository, owner, and PR number automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Accept GitHub PR URLs in the format https://github.com/{owner}/{repo}/pull/{number}.
Reject invalid URLs with a clear validation message.
Extract owner, repo, and pull request number into a typed internal model.
Show parsed PR metadata preview before starting analysis.
Backend tests pass.
Frontend typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Fetch PR metadata and diff
userStories.description: As a user, I want the assistant to retrieve PR changes from GitHub so that I do not need to copy code manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Use GitHub API to fetch PR title, author, base branch, head branch, changed files, additions, deletions, and commit count.
Fetch unified diffs for changed files and preserve file paths and line ranges.
Handle private repositories when a valid GitHub token is configured.
Return actionable errors for missing token, unauthorized access, rate limits, and not found PRs.
Add a mock GitHub provider for demo and tests.
Backend tests pass.

userStories.title: Collect relevant code context
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want the assistant to inspect context around changed code so that review suggestions are grounded beyond the raw diff.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetch current file content for changed files when available.
Extract nearby function or class context around modified hunks using language-aware heuristics where possible.
Identify likely related files such as tests, schemas, API routes, and configuration files by path patterns.
Limit context size with deterministic token or character budgets per file.
Include context source labels so generated findings can cite evidence.
Backend tests pass.

userStories.title: Generate PR change summary
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want a concise PR summary so that I can quickly understand what changed and where to focus.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate a summary covering business intent, key changed modules, and notable implementation details.
Group changed files by category such as backend, frontend, tests, config, docs, or unknown.
Return structured JSON containing overview, file groups, and reviewer focus areas.
When model output is malformed, retry once and then return a graceful fallback summary.
Backend tests pass.

userStories.title: Detect risky code changes
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want risky code changes to be highlighted so that I can prioritize correctness, security, and maintainability issues.
acceptanceCriteria: Analyze changed code across risk categories: correctness, security, performance, compatibility, observability, data handling, and missing tests.
Assign each finding a severity of critical, high, medium, or low.
Each finding includes file path, line reference when available, evidence, reasoning, and confidence score.
Suppress low-confidence findings below the configured threshold from the default view.
Return an empty findings list with explanation when no meaningful risks are detected.
Backend tests pass.

userStories.title: Generate actionable review suggestions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want review comments that explain the problem and suggest a fix so that I can act on them quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate review suggestions tied to specific findings and changed files.
Each suggestion includes comment text, rationale, suggested fix, and whether it should block merge.
Avoid generic comments that do not cite code evidence.
Provide test suggestions for changed behavior and identified risk areas.
Support Chinese output by default with an option-ready response field for future language switching.
Backend tests pass.

userStories.title: Build LangGraph review workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the review analysis split into observable workflow nodes so that accuracy, latency, and failures can be managed independently.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement workflow nodes for input validation, PR fetch, context collection, summary generation, risk analysis, suggestion generation, and final report assembly.
Persist task status in memory or lightweight storage with states: queued, running, succeeded, failed.
Expose structured node progress events for the frontend.
Allow failed model nodes to return partial results when earlier steps succeeded.
Backend tests pass.

userStories.title: Stream review progress
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see review progress in real time so that the tool feels responsive during longer PR analysis.
acceptanceCriteria: Expose a review task creation endpoint that returns a task ID.
Expose an SSE or WebSocket endpoint that streams node progress, warnings, partial summary, partial findings, and completion events.
Frontend displays current analysis stage and progressively renders available results.
Connection loss can be recovered by reloading task status with the task ID.
Backend tests pass.
Frontend typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Create PR review dashboard
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want a focused dashboard for summary, risks, and suggestions so that I can review findings efficiently.
acceptanceCriteria: Create an input panel for GitHub PR URL and analysis start action.
Show PR metadata, changed file counts, additions, deletions, and branch information.
Show summary, risk findings, review suggestions, and test recommendations in separate scan-friendly sections.
Allow filtering findings by severity and category.
Display file path and line reference for each finding when available.
Frontend typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Add simulated publishing of review comments
userStories.description: As a demo user, I want to preview comments that would be posted to GitHub so that the tool can demonstrate one-click review without requiring write permissions.
acceptanceCriteria: Provide a simulated publish action that marks selected suggestions as prepared review comments.
Render a GitHub-style preview containing file path, line reference, and comment body.
Do not call GitHub write APIs in simulation mode.
Design the provider interface so real GitHub review submission can be added later.
Backend tests pass.
Frontend typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Add accuracy controls and guardrails
userStories.description: As a reviewer, I want the assistant to control hallucination and noisy findings so that I can trust the output.
acceptanceCriteria: Require every high or critical finding to include concrete code evidence from diff or fetched context.
Add confidence scoring and a configurable minimum confidence threshold.
Add a final self-check step that removes duplicate, unsupported, or overly broad findings.
Show a disclaimer when analysis is partial due to missing context, API limits, or model failure.
Include prompt fixtures or golden examples for regression testing major output shapes.
Backend tests pass.

userStories.title: Document model and context strategy
userStories.description: As an evaluator, I want to understand model selection, context acquisition, and extension direction so that the project design can be judged beyond the demo.
acceptanceCriteria: Document recommended model strategy: fast model for summary and stronger model for risk analysis.
Document context strategy using PR diff, changed file content, nearby symbols, related tests, and repository metadata.
Document false positive and false negative control methods.
Document latency optimizations such as file-level batching, streaming, and partial results.
Document future extensions for GitLab, Gitee, organization rules, CI integration, and inline GitHub review submission.
Docs are linked from README.

userStories.title: Create competition-ready README
userStories.description: As an evaluator, I want a complete README so that I can understand, run, and assess the submitted work without extra explanation.
acceptanceCriteria: README states the selected topic direction and explains why the work fits AI PR Review assistant requirements.
README includes product overview, target users, core features, architecture diagram or architecture section, and usage flow.
README includes local setup, environment variables, dependency installation, backend startup, frontend startup, and demo mode instructions.
README lists all third-party libraries, frameworks, model providers, and external APIs used by the project.
README clearly identifies original implementation work and cites any reused code, templates, or references.
README links to model and context strategy documentation.
Backend tests pass.
Frontend typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Define PR and commit delivery workflow
userStories.description: As a participant, I want the repository workflow to satisfy the competition delivery rules so that the work is considered valid.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a delivery checklist documenting the 3-day schedule: day 1 repository creation and topic submission, day 2 iterative PR and commit delivery, day 3 final code, README, and demo video submission.
Document that all commits must occur within the official start and deadline window.
Document that features should be submitted through PRs rather than one final bulk commit.
Document that each PR should implement or modify one focused feature.
Document the required PR description template: title, feature description, implementation approach, and testing method.
Document that the main branch must remain runnable after PR merge.
Backend tests pass.
Frontend typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Add demo presentation materials
userStories.description: As an evaluator, I want a clear demo video and presentation outline so that I can quickly understand the product functions and effects.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a demo script covering problem background, target users, product workflow, PR analysis result, risk finding detail, simulated review comment publishing, and extension plan.
Create a demo checklist to ensure the video shows a complete successful PR analysis from input URL to final report.
Prepare sample PR data or mock data that can be analyzed reliably during recording.
Demo materials explain the scoring focus: product completeness and innovation, development quality, and presentation clarity.
README links to demo video location or placeholder before final submission.
Backend tests pass.
Frontend typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Add final validity and scoring checklist
userStories.description: As a participant, I want a final submission checklist so that avoidable invalidation risks are caught before the deadline.
acceptanceCriteria: Checklist verifies the work fits the selected topic and has no plagiarism risk.
Checklist verifies PR descriptions are not empty and match actual code changes.
Checklist verifies third-party dependencies are declared in README.
Checklist verifies any reused past code is cited in PR descriptions or README.
Checklist verifies repository is public or otherwise accessible to evaluators.
Checklist verifies final main branch can run and demonstrates the expected product effect.
Checklist verifies final submission includes public code repository, README, and demo video.
Backend tests pass.
Frontend typecheck passes.
